steps.title: Set up and simplify inequalities
steps.content:
For a valid triangle, the sum of any two sides exceeds the third:

$$7 + 10 > x \quad \Rightarrow \quad x < 17$$

$$7 + x > 10 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x > 3$$

$$10 + x > 7 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x > -3 \text{ (redundant)}$$

steps.title: Combine constraints
steps.content: Both $x > 3$ and $x < 17$ must hold simultaneously. Combined: $3 < x < 17$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
